---
title: "tree"
format: html
engine: julia
execute:
  daemon: false
julia:
  exeflags: ["--project=.@"]
---

# tree

## basic structure

```{bash}
Any
├─ Built-in types
│   ├─ abstract type
│   │  ├─ Number
│   │   ├─ Complex
│   │   └─ Real
│   │       ├─ AbstractFloat
│   │       │  ├─ Float16
│   │       │  ├─ Float32
│   │       │  ├─ Float64
│   │       │  └─ BigFloat
│   │       ├─ Integer
│   │       │  ├─ Bool
│   │       │  ├─ Signed
│   │       │  │  ├─ Int8 / Int16 / Int32 / Int64 / Int128 / BigInt
│   │       │  └─ Unsigned
│   │       │     ├─ UInt8 / UInt16 / UInt32 / UInt64 / UInt128
│   │       ├─ Rational
│   │       └─ AbstractIrrational
│   │  ├─ AbstractString
│   │  ├─ AbstractArray{T,N}
│   │  └─ ...
├─ Custom type
```

## built-in types

```{julia}
x = 1
typeof(x) # which return the specific type of x, in this case, it is Int64, which is a subtype of Integer,
x isa Int64 # judge whether x is an instance of Int64.
Int64 <: Signed # judge whether Int64 is a subtype of Signed.
```

## custom type

```{julia}
# immutable struct.
struct Person
    name::String
    age::Int
end

p = Person("Tom", 20)
p.age = 21   # which is error, you can not change the value of age, because it is immutable.
```

```{julia}
# mutable struct.
mutable struct Person
    name::String
    age::Int
end

p = Person("Tom", 20)
p.age = 21   # 可以
println(p.age)   # 21
```

```{julia}
# abstract type is to difine a abstract classification, containing some structs or other abstract types.
# it can not be instantiated to store specific data.
abstract type Animal end

struct Dog <: Animal
    name::String
end

d = Dog("Bob")
```
